title: SQSS shared primitives
description: Shared scalar/value primitives referenced by the SQSS wire types. Realizes SPECIFICATION.md §3 (Conventions) and §4 (Data model). Non-normative prose lives in the spec; these schemas are the authoritative shapes.

PartyId.title: PartyId
PartyId.description: A Canton party identifier (SPEC §4.1). Opaque non-empty string; SQSS does not constrain the internal format beyond non-emptiness so as not to diverge from Canton's own party-id format.

VenueId.title: VenueId
VenueId.description: Stable identifier of a liquidity venue/adapter (SPEC §2, §4.3).

Timestamp.title: Timestamp
Timestamp.description: Absolute UTC instant, RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time (SPEC §3: time is UTC; deadlines and validity are absolute timestamps).

Decimal.title: Decimal
Decimal.description: Exact decimal value carried as a string to avoid binary floating point for value math (SPEC §3). Canonical form: optional leading '-', no leading zeros (except '0'), optional fractional part with at least one digit. No exponent notation.

Signature.title: Signature
Signature.description: Opaque venue signature over a firm quote (SPEC §4.3). REQUIRED when a quote is firm. Encoding is venue/registry defined; SQSS treats it as an opaque non-empty token to be verified against the venue's key out of band / on-ledger.

CommitmentRef.title: CommitmentRef
CommitmentRef.description: Reference to an on-ledger commitment that backs a firm quote and can be honored during CIP-0056 settlement (SPEC §4.3, §6). REQUIRED when a quote is firm.

AssetId.title: AssetId
AssetId.description: Registry-qualified instrument identifier consistent with CIP-0056 (SPEC §3, RFC-0001 Decision A: the issuing registry plus the instrument, never a symbol alone). Exactly three fields: registry, instrumentId, decimals.

registry.description: The issuing registry / token administrator identifier (the authority; a Canton party).

instrumentId.description: Instrument identifier within the issuing registry.

decimals.description: The instrument's defined decimal precision (non-negative). NOT authoritative on its own: it MUST be consistent with the precision the issuing registry reports via the CIP-0056 token metadata API, and is carried here only so amounts can be validated off-ledger without global readable state (ARCHITECTURE.md §1 #2). Amounts of this asset MUST NOT carry more fractional digits than this.

NetworkFee.title: NetworkFee
NetworkFee.description: A flat or gas-like cost the taker bears for a swap, in its native asset (SPEC §4.3; RFC-0005 §2; RFC-0006): the on-ledger equivalent of gas. Absent means the venue discloses no separate network fee. The `appliedTo` direction defaults to on_top (the taker pays the fee in addition to the give, RFC-0005 behavior); deducted_from_give means the fee is taken from within the give before pricing. The asset MUST equal the quote's give.asset or its receive.asset, and MUST equal the give.asset when appliedTo is deducted_from_give; a fee denominated in a third asset needs an external price to value it and is deferred to a follow-up RFC. `amount` is non-negative.

asset.description: The asset the network fee is denominated in. MUST equal the quote's give.asset or its receive.asset (RFC-0005 §2); MUST equal the give.asset when appliedTo is deducted_from_give (RFC-0006 §2).

appliedTo.description: How the fee relates to the give the taker parts with (RFC-0006). Absent means on_top: the taker pays the fee in addition to the give (RFC-0005 behavior). deducted_from_give means the fee is taken from within the give before the swap is priced, so the delivered receive is already net of it; in that case the asset MUST equal the quote's give.asset.
